typecheck.command: python -c "import pathlib, py_compile; [py_compile.compile(str(path), doraise=True) for path in pathlib.Path('src').rglob('*.py')]; print('py_compile ok')"

blockingIssues.description: The fix makes rookie fallback and roster-turnover wiring reachable for live offense/pitching/defense callers, but `src/model/data_builder.py:735` now threads `roster_turnover_by_team` through historical rebuilds that still default to `_empty_lineups_fetcher()`. When lineups are absent, `_build_roster_turnover_by_team()` falls back to starter-only current rosters (`src/model/data_builder.py:824-837`), so training, calibration, stacking, and walk-forward rebuilds can halve prior weights for offense/defense based on starter continuity rather than actual >40% roster turnover.

The fix removes the original picks-then-failure double-webhook path, but `_apply_pick_side_effects()` in `src/pipeline/daily.py:1009` still commits `update_bankroll()` before `freeze_odds()` / settlement. If a later side effect fails, the run downgrades the game to an error and suppresses the pick while leaving the bet and bankroll ledger mutation committed, creating a hidden placed bet / bankroll debit that is missing from the pipeline results and webhook output.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/workspace-state.md` to reflect that `fix-t24-live-features` restored fresh same-day feature assembly via `build_live_feature_frame()` and no longer uses the old persisted-row fast path for live runs.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Fix or replace the shared `init_windows` command with a tested Windows-safe init command; the current inline `init_db(''data/mlb.db'')` quoting is unreliable in PowerShell and continues to force worker startup deviations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review `fix-t24-live-features` flagged `.factory/services.yaml:7`, and this scrutiny run independently had to bypass the manifest command after reproducing quote-related failures while initializing the repo on Windows.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Call out that `src/model/data_builder.py::_compute_feature_modules()` is shared by both live inference and historical rebuilds, and require verification of both paths when feature-module wiring changes land there.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review `fix-t25-first-year-roster-turnover` showed the live-path blocker was fixed, but the same shared data-builder change regressed training/backtest rebuilds because the worker only validated the live path with lineups present.
